summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: a `spec_features` extractor + embed `contracts/spec.toml`, running admissibility and `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it cleanly

edit.description: Add `spec_features(&Spec) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features` (id = name, `body_lines`, `headings`, `source_dir`; no fields/companions).

Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features, parse the spec floor, run `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them, and merge into the diagnostic stream.

tests.asserts: `spec_features` exposes the spec id, body line count, and ATX headings; the `max_lines` clause fires (advisory) on an over-length spec and stays silent within budget.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs (00, 10) at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: also requires SPEC-KIND-WORKSPACE (for `Workspace.specs`); `spec_features` is added here. Runs `contracts/spec.toml`'s active `max_lines` clause only — its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await separate predicates (see `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier).
